name: Mushroom and Leek Crepes
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups Thinly sliced or small-diced Domestic mushrooms
1 tablespoon Italian parsley or mixed herbs
1/3 cup Masa harina (a type of corn flour)
Freshly chopped parsley or other herb for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. For the filling: Braise leeks in white wine for 20 minutes. Add domestic and fresh shiitake mushrooms, balsamic vinegar to the mushrooms, and cook until all liquid has been released and is mostly reduced. Season with salt and pepper before the mushrooms are completely cooked. Toss with parsley or mixed herbs.
2. There should be about 5 cups of filling.
3. For the crepe batter: Sift flour and masa harina together. Mix in water until a light, creamy batter forms that coats a wooden spoon. If the batter is lumpy, blend it in a blender. Make a test crepe by ladling about 3 ounces of batter onto a nonstick skillet. Cook until golden on both sides. The crepe should be very thin and flexible; if not, add more water and mix again. There should be about 3 cups of batter, enough for approximately 14 crepes.
4. For the sauce: Add shiitake mushrooms, onions, and domestic mushrooms to water in a large pot. Cook for 30-45 minutes until the onions are soft and some liquid is reduced. Remove the onions and mushrooms using a slotted spoon, reserving about 1 1/2 cups of liquid. Add 2 cups red wine to the stock and cook on very high heat for a few minutes to reduce alcohol. Gradually whisk semolina flour into the simmering hot stock until thickened but still pourable. There should be about 2 2/3 cups of sauce.
5. Preheat oven to 375°F. For each crepe, fill with approximately 1/3 cup filling and top with 1 1/2 tablespoons sauce. Roll up the crepes and place them in a baking dish. Repeat until all 14 crepes are made. Drizzle additional sauce over the crepes and bake for 10 minutes or until hot. Garnish with chopped parsley or fresh herbs before serving.
